What it does: This skill runs an interactive Q&A session. It asks you about your product category, your monthly ad budget, your business goals (sales, awareness, leads), your pixel maturity stage, and your current winners (if any). Based on your answers, it generates a complete media buying strategy document that includes:

  • A creative-to-link mapping table with UTM parameters
  • A testing matrix using the 3-2-2 framework, customized for your budget
  • Budget allocation across the three buckets, with daily and weekly breakdowns
  • KPI targets based on your category and market
  • The exact Claude Code commands to create each campaign, pull each report, and execute each optimization

You can run this skill at the start of each month to refresh your strategy, or whenever you launch a new product line or enter a new selling season.

What You Have Now

Step back. Look at what you built across thirteen sections.

Foundation (Sections 1-4): - Claude Code installed and configured on your machine - Shopify fully connected -- you can read and modify products, collections, prices, inventory, and metafields through plain English commands - Meta fully connected -- you can create campaigns, manage ad sets, upload catalog feeds, and pull performance insights through the same interface

Tooling (Sections 5-6): - Playwright plugin for browser automation and visual verification - A memory system that accumulates knowledge across sessions - An error handling framework that learns from every failure - A self-improvement cycle that makes the entire system smarter over time

Knowledge (Sections 7-8): - The ability to read and understand API documentation - The ability to write Standard Operating Procedures from API knowledge - A structured approach to turning manual processes into documented, repeatable steps

Operations (Sections 9-10): - 10 SOPs covering your most important daily tasks: campaign creation, budget management, performance analysis, catalog updates, price management, collection sorting, and more - Each SOP is a tested, documented procedure that Claude Code can execute on command

Going Agentic (Sections 11-13): - 17 Claude Skills that turn SOPs into one-command automations - 3 Agentic workflows that chain multiple operations into end-to-end processes - A media buying strategy approach powered by Claude Code -- build your own testing plans, budget allocations, and analysis loops

What used to take hours of clicking, copy-pasting, and manual tracking is now a conversation in plain English. Your next campaign is one sentence away.

KEY TAKEAWAY
Strategy isn't set-and-forget. The test → analyze → optimize loop runs continuously. Claude Code handles the execution, but you provide the judgment.
REAL TALK
Professional media buyers don't follow one-size-fits-all templates. They build strategies from data, test systematically, and optimize continuously. With Claude Code, you have the same analytical power and execution speed they do — minus the agency fees.

Quick Troubleshooting

Problem Solution
UTM parameters are not showing in analytics Verify the full URL is being used as the destination. Some ad formats strip query parameters. Check the ad preview in Ads Manager to confirm the URL includes the ?utm_source=... portion.
ROAS data shows zero despite having sales Meta attributes conversions based on your pixel and attribution window. Make sure your pixel is firing correctly (use Meta Pixel Helper browser extension). Check that your attribution setting matches your expectation (default is 7-day click, 1-day view).
Budget increase triggered learning phase reset You likely increased by more than 20%. Let the learning phase complete (usually 3-5 days), then make smaller incremental changes. Save this lesson to memory so Claude Code avoids recommending large jumps.
Campaign Strategy Builder generates irrelevant recommendations The skill relies on your answers during the interactive session. Be specific about your product category, budget constraints, and pixel maturity. Vague inputs produce generic outputs.
Weekly analysis takes too long to run If you have many campaigns, the Meta API calls can take time. Use the insights endpoint with level=campaign first for a high-level view, then drill into specific campaigns as needed. Claude Code caches responses within a session.
All test variations are below ROAS target This is a signal, not a failure. Review your offer (is the discount compelling enough?), your creative (is it thumb-stopping?), and your landing page (does it load fast? is checkout smooth?). Sometimes the issue is upstream of the ad itself.

What Is Next

There is no Section 14. This is the end of the playbook -- but it is the beginning of your system.

The framework you have built is not static. It improves every time you use it. Every campaign you run adds to your memory. Every error you encounter makes the system stronger. Every analysis cycle sharpens your strategy.

Start with one campaign. Use the Campaign Strategy Builder to plan it. Let Claude Code create it. Wait a week. Run the analysis. Make decisions. Execute them. Save what you learned.

Then do it again.

The compound effect of automated execution, systematic testing, and institutional memory is extraordinary. Six months from now, you will look back at the manual, click-by-click way you used to work and wonder how you ever had the time.

ACHIEVEMENT UNLOCKED
Playbook complete. You've built a fully connected, self-improving, agentic marketing system — 13 sections, 10 SOPs, 17 skills, 3 workflows, and a strategy framework. From here, it only gets better with use.

You have the playbook. You have the tools. Go build something.

Your Roadmap Forward

You have finished the playbook. Everything is set up, connected, and working. Now what?

Here is the honest truth: this playbook is a snapshot in time. The APIs you just learned will change. Shopify will release new GraphQL versions. Meta will deprecate endpoints and add new ones. Claude Code itself will get new features, new models, and new capabilities. The screenshots in this guide will look different in six months.

That is not a problem. It is actually the point.

What This Playbook Gave You

This playbook taught you the technical know-how -- how APIs work, how authentication flows, how to structure SOPs, how to build skills, how to chain workflows. That knowledge does not expire when an API version increments. The patterns stay the same even when the details change.

You now understand:

  • How OAuth and client credentials authentication works (not just for Shopify -- for any API)
  • How to read API documentation and translate it into working automations
  • How to structure repeatable procedures that anyone can follow
  • How to build skills that turn complex operations into single commands
  • How to chain operations into end-to-end workflows
  • How to use Claude Code as an execution layer between you and any API

These are transferable skills. They work for Meta today, but they also work for TikTok Ads, Google Ads, Klaviyo, or whatever platform you add next.

When Things Change (And They Will)

Here is how to handle the inevitable updates:

API version changes. Shopify and Meta release new API versions every few months. When they do, tell Claude Code:

"Check if there are any breaking changes between Shopify API version 2025-01 and 2025-04. Update my SOPs if anything changed."

Claude Code will read the changelog, identify what affects your setup, and update your skills accordingly.

New features. When a platform adds a new feature you want to use, you do not need to wait for a playbook update. Tell Claude Code:

"Meta just launched a new ad format called [name]. Read the documentation and build me an SOP for it."

You already know how SOPs work. You already have the SOP Builder skill. Creating a new one for a new feature takes minutes, not days.

Errors you have never seen before. APIs break in new and creative ways. When you hit an error that is not in your troubleshooting tables, tell Claude Code:

"I'm getting this error: [paste the error]. Figure out what's wrong and fix it."

Your Error Handler skill and memory system mean that every error you solve once is solved forever.

The Path to Independence

Here is the progression most people follow:

Month 1-2: Follow the playbook. Use the skills and SOPs exactly as written. Copy-paste the prompts. Get comfortable with the workflow. Build confidence that the system works.

Month 3-4: Adapt and customize. Start modifying skills for your specific needs. Create new SOPs for tasks the playbook did not cover. Your memory system is accumulating patterns specific to your store and your market.

Month 5-6: Build your own. You stop needing the playbook entirely. You see a new marketing challenge and think, "I can build a skill for that." You encounter a new API and know exactly how to connect it. You design workflows from scratch because you understand the patterns.

Month 7+: You are the expert. You are building systems that the playbook never imagined. Your Claude Code setup has months of accumulated memory, dozens of custom skills, and workflows tailored to your exact business. You are not following a guide anymore -- you are writing your own.

The goal was never to make you dependent on this playbook. The goal was to teach you the patterns so you could outgrow it.

Your Scenarios Will Be Different

Every store is different. Your products, your market, your customers, your budget, your team -- all of it shapes the workflows you need. The playbook showed you one path through the landscape, but there are hundreds of valid paths.

Maybe you sell digital products and do not need inventory SOPs. Maybe you run ads on TikTok instead of Meta. Maybe you have a team of five and need collaborative workflows instead of solo ones. Maybe you are in a market where WhatsApp matters more than Instagram.

All of those scenarios are valid. And now you have the building blocks to handle all of them. The SOPs, skills, and workflows in this playbook are templates. Take what works. Modify what almost works. Build from scratch what does not exist yet.

Get Help When You Need It

You are not alone in this. If you hit a blocker, get stuck on an API issue, or just want to talk through a workflow idea, reach out:

Meska AI Community Join the Meska AI group to connect with other store owners using the same system. Share workflows, troubleshoot together, and learn from each other's experiments.

Direct Contact Reach out to me directly on Instagram: instagram.com/itsmazinzaki

Whether it is a technical blocker, a strategic question, or an idea you want to bounce -- I am here to help. The whole point of building this community is that nobody has to figure it out alone.

Appendices

Appendix A: Complete Skill Inventory

# Skill Name Section Commands Download
1 Project Scaffolder Ch 3 /setup-project Download
2 Shopify Connector Ch 3 /shopify-connect, /shopify-test Download
3 Meta Ads Connector Ch 4 /meta-connect, /meta-test Download
4 Plugin Setup Wizard Ch 5 /setup-plugins Download
5 Error Handler Ch 6 /handle-error Download
6 Memory Manager Ch 6 /memory save\|search\|audit\|export Download
7 Health Check Ch 6 /health-check Download
8 Meta Docs Navigator Ch 7 /read-meta-docs Download
9 SOP Builder Ch 8 /build-sop Download
10 Media Buying SOP Pack Ch 9 /create-campaign, /setup-adset, /create-ad, /check-performance, /scale-campaign Download
11 Meta Strategy Builder Ch 9 /meta-strategy test\|scale\|funnel\|audit Download
12 Shopify Jobs SOP Pack Ch 10 /list-products, /check-orders, /check-inventory, /update-prices, /manage-collections Download
13 Collections Mapper Ch 10 /collections-map\|build\|audit Download
14 Job Runner Ch 10 /shopify-job daily\|weekly\|custom Download
15 SOP-to-Skill Converter Ch 11 /sop-to-skill Download
16 Workflow Orchestrator Ch 12 /workflow launch-product\|daily-ops\|low-stock-react Download
17 Campaign Strategy Builder Ch 13 /campaign-strategy Download

Installation: Copy each skill's SKILL.md file into your project's .claude/skills/<skill-name>/ directory.

Appendix B: Complete .env Template

# ============================================
# SHOPIFY CONFIGURATION
# Dev Dashboard - Client Credentials Grant
# ============================================

# Your store name (without .myshopify.com)
SHOPIFY_SHOP=your-store-name

# From Dev Dashboard > Your App > Settings
SHOPIFY_CLIENT_ID=your-client-id
SHOPIFY_CLIENT_SECRET=your-client-secret

# ============================================
# META / FACEBOOK CONFIGURATION
# ============================================

# Long-lived User Access Token (60 days)
# Generated via Graph API Explorer, then extended
META_ACCESS_TOKEN=your-long-lived-token

# Ad Account ID (include the act_ prefix!)
META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID=act_your_account_id

# From Meta App Dashboard > Settings > Basic
META_APP_ID=your-app-id
META_APP_SECRET=your-app-secret

# Your Facebook Page ID
META_PAGE_ID=your-page-id

# Your Meta Pixel ID
META_PIXEL_ID=your-pixel-id

# ============================================
# OPTIONAL CONFIGURATION
# ============================================

# Meta Product Catalog ID (if using DPA)
# META_CATALOG_ID=your-catalog-id

# Instagram Account ID (if running IG ads)
# META_INSTAGRAM_ID=your-instagram-id

# Your store's currency code
# CURRENCY=EGP

# Your timezone
# TIMEZONE=Africa/Cairo

Security reminder: Never commit this file to version control. The .gitignore created by the Project Scaffolder skill already excludes .env files.

Appendix C: Complete .mcp.json Template

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@anthropic-ai/mcp-playwright@latest"],
      "env": {}
    }
  }
}

Where this file lives: In your project root directory as .mcp.json.

Adding more plugins: Each plugin gets its own entry inside the mcpServers object. Use the /setup-plugins skill (Section 5) to configure your plugins interactively.

Verifying plugins: After saving this file, restart Claude Code and type /mcp to see all active plugins and their status.

Appendix D: Screenshot Placeholders Guide

The following screenshots require manual capture because they show private dashboards and admin panels. Each entry lists the filename, where to navigate, and exactly what the screenshot should show.

Shopify Screenshots (5)

# Filename Navigate To What to Capture
1 shopify-admin-settings-apps.png Shopify Admin > Settings > Apps Apps list with "Develop apps" button visible
2 shopify-dev-dashboard-create-app.png dev.shopify.com > Create App App creation dialog with name field
3 shopify-dev-dashboard-app-credentials.png Dev Dashboard > App > Settings Client ID and Client Secret fields
4 shopify-dev-dashboard-distribution.png Dev Dashboard > App > Distribution Distribution method selection (Custom)
5 shopify-dev-dashboard-install-confirm.png Install link for your store Permission scopes and Install button

Meta Screenshots (5)

# Filename Navigate To What to Capture
6 meta-app-dashboard-create.png developers.facebook.com > Create App App type selection and name fields
7 meta-app-dashboard-privacy-live.png App Dashboard > Settings > Basic Privacy Policy URL field + Live mode toggle
8 meta-graph-api-explorer-permissions.png Tools > Graph API Explorer Permission checkboxes (ads_management, etc.)
9 meta-graph-api-explorer-token.png Graph API Explorer after token generation Token field populated + successful response
10 meta-business-settings-system-users.png Business Settings > System Users System user list and Add button

Claude Code Screenshots (3)

# Filename Navigate To What to Capture
11 claude-code-first-launch.png Terminal after running claude Welcome message and version info
12 claude-code-browser-auth.png Terminal during auth flow "Opening browser..." message
13 claude-code-api-success.png Terminal after successful command Working response showing connection

How to Capture

  1. Mac: Cmd + Shift + 4 for selected area
  2. Windows: Win + Shift + S for Snipping Tool
  3. Format: Save as PNG
  4. Sensitive data: Blur API keys, tokens, and secrets
  5. Save to: docs/playbook/screenshots/

The Agentic Ecom Playbook — From Manual Marketing to Agentic Workflows with Claude Code
